question: Is Novo Nordisk's revenue decline (-7.6% YoY) temporary or structural?

findings.claim: CagriSema achieved 22.7% weight loss at 68 weeks in REDEFINE 1 trial — better than semaglutide alone (14%) but fell short of 25% target
findings.data: REDEFINE 1 (3,417 adults with obesity, no diabetes): CagriSema achieved 20.4% mean weight reduction vs 3.0% placebo (ITT). On-treatment estimate: 22.7%. Weight loss >=20%: 60.2%, >=25%: 40.4%, >=30%: 23.1% of patients. Published in NEJM. GI adverse events: 79.6% vs 39.9% placebo. Investors were disappointed as ~25% had been the expectation — shares fell on announcement.

findings.claim: CagriSema NDA filed with FDA in December 2025, approval expected late 2026 or early 2027
findings.data: Novo filed NDA for CagriSema on December 18, 2025. CagriSema is a fixed-dose combination of semaglutide 2.4mg + cagrilintide 2.4mg (amylin analog). REDEFINE 2 (T2D patients): 13.7% weight loss, 73.5% reaching HbA1c <=6.5%. Head-to-head trial vs Zepbound ongoing. REDEFINE 11 trial initiated for longer-duration study.

findings.claim: Novo initiated Phase 1 study for LX9851, an oral non-incretin obesity candidate, with Lexicon Pharmaceuticals
findings.data: On March 23, 2026, Novo Nordisk and Lexicon Pharmaceuticals announced initiation of a Phase 1 study with LX9851, an oral non-incretin development candidate for obesity and metabolic disorders. This diversifies Novo's pipeline beyond GLP-1 mechanisms.

findings.claim: Semaglutide failed to show benefit in early Alzheimer's disease trial, narrowing its beyond-obesity potential
findings.data: At AD/PD 2026 conference, Novo Nordisk's semaglutide failed to demonstrate any benefit for early Alzheimer's disease. This was a setback for the thesis that semaglutide could expand into large neurological indications beyond metabolic disease.
